summary: Cathy O'Neil confesses to having an enormous yarn collection that had been scattered throughout her house in bags. With help from a professional organizer friend, she finally consolidated and organized her stash. Rather than feeling embarrassed by the sheer volume—which she estimates rivals an average yarn shop—she embraces the beauty of her collection and the joy her knitting hobby has brought her over the years.
key_points: Her yarn collection had grown so large it was hidden in bags throughout every corner of her home
She tends to buy excess yarn for projects and keeps the leftovers, plus purchases sale yarn she hasn't yet used
The organization process led to an appreciation of her collection's beauty rather than shame
She jokes the stash could function as a backup yarn shop if she ever needs money
money_quotes: I have a deeply alarming amount of yarn, which has hitherto been gathered in smallish bags tucked all over the house, in every nook, cranny, and corner.
I have officially tamed the yarn beast.
I always buy a bit too much yarn and then keep the extra.

I am beyond shame and embarrassment at this point, because at some point, when all my yarn was splayed across my entire living room and dining room, I realized how amazingly beautiful it all is and how much I've gotten out of my hobby over the years.
I think I might actually have more yarn than the average yarn shop.
Life is too short to be ashamed of your passion.
key_insight: Embracing your passions fully—even when they seem excessive to others—can bring unexpected joy and appreciation.
